prompt: Create a multi-agent content production workflow triggered by a webhook POST at /content-request containing a topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social). First, a Research Agent with tools for web search and knowledge base lookup gathers background information and outputs a research brief with key facts and sources. Second, a Strategy Agent with tools for SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking produces a content plan with outline and keyword strategy. Third, route based on content type: blog posts go to a Writing Agent with tools for draft generation and readability scoring; social media goes to a Social Media Agent with tools for platform formatting (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation that produces 3 post variations. Fourth, all paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with tools for grammar checking and fact verification that outputs a quality score. If the score is below 85%, loop back to the writing agent with revision notes for up to 3 cycles. Fifth, approved content goes to a human approval webhook wait — send a Slack message with the content and wait for a webhook callback. If approved, publish to WordPress and archive to Google Drive. Store all outputs in Airtable.

checklist.item: Has a webhook POST trigger at /content-request
Webhook payload contains topic, target audience, and content type (blog or social)
Research Agent is the first agent in the pipeline, equipped with web search and knowledge base lookup tools
Research Agent outputs a research brief containing key facts and sources
Strategy Agent runs second, equipped with SEO keyword analysis and content calendar checking tools
Strategy Agent produces a content plan with an outline and keyword strategy
Workflow branches based on content type field: blog path and social media path
Blog path routes to a Writing Agent with draft generation and readability scoring tools
Social media path routes to a Social Media Agent with platform formatting tools (Twitter/LinkedIn) and hashtag generation tools
Social Media Agent produces 3 post variations
Both content type paths converge to an Editorial Review Agent with grammar checking and fact verification tools
Editorial Review Agent outputs a quality score
If quality score is below 85%, workflow loops back to the writing agent with revision notes, for a maximum of 3 revision cycles
Approved content (score >= 85%) enters a human approval step: sends a Slack message with the content and waits for a webhook callback response
If human approves via webhook callback, content is published to WordPress and archived to a cloud file storage service (Google Drive)
All agent outputs are stored in a persistent data table (Airtable)
